title: quercus aliena f. jeholensis
intro: Quercus aliena f. jeholensis, also known as the Jehol Oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus aliena plant, native to China. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties attributed to its bark and leaves, which have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects. The Jehol Oak is believed to have various health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herb for respiratory and cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Quercus aliena f. jeholensis are typically dried and processed into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
